explanation: Shah Nawaz Khan, Prem Kumar Sehgal, and Gurbaksh Singh Dhillon were prominent officers in the Indian National Army (INA) led by Subhas Chandra Bose. Their trial by the British after World War II became a significant event in the Indian independence movement, galvanizing public support for the INA.

The black cotton soil, also known as Regur soil, is primarily formed from the weathering of fissure volcanic rocks, particularly basaltic lava flows. This process releases minerals rich in iron and lime, which contribute to the characteristic black color and fertility of these soils.
